```python
import openpyxl
from openpyxl.chart import BarChart, Reference
import argparse

def create_chart(sheet, data_range, title):
    chart = BarChart()
    chart.title = title
    chart.add_data(data_range, titles_from_data=True)
    chart.y_axis.title = "Values"
    chart.x_axis.title = "Categories"
    
    # Set the font for the chart
    chart.graphicalProperties.line.solidFill = "FF0000"  # Example line color
    chart.graphicalProperties.font.size = 12  # Example font size
    chart.graphicalProperties.font.bold = True  # Example bold setting
    chart.graphicalProperties.font.typeface = "Credit Suisse Type Light"  # Set the font

    return chart

def generate_report(country):
    wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
    sheet = wb.active
    sheet.title = country

    # Sample data for demonstration
    data = [
        ['Month', 'Value'],
        ['January', 100],
        ['February', 200],
        ['March', 300],
    ]
    
    for row in data:
        sheet.append(row)

    data_range = Reference(sheet, min_col=2, min_row=1, max_col=2, max_row=len(data))
    chart = create_chart(sheet, data_range, f"{country} Monthly Report")
    
    sheet.add_chart(chart, "E5")
    wb.save(f"{country}_monthly_report.xlsx")

def solve(*args):
    if len(args) != 1:
        raise ValueError("Expected one argument: country name")
    country = args[0]
    generate_report(country)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Generate monthly report for a country.')
    parser.add_argument('-c', '--country', required=True, help='Country name')
    args = parser.parse_args()
    solve(args.country)
```
